title: Introduction
description: NOC 63200 – Cooks | GTR Immigration Cooks under NOC 63200 are the vital culinary professionals powering Canada’s diverse food service industry. Unlike chefs, who primarily focus on management and menu design, cooks are the hands-on experts responsible for preparing and presenting a wide variety of meals across restaurants, hospitals, and specialized campsites. As a TEER 3 occupation, this role is a cornerstone of the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP) and is a top-tier candidate for Category-Based Selection draws within the Express Entry system. At GTR Immigration, we help skilled cooks transition from international kitchens to permanent residency in Canada.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level3Major Group63 – Occupations in sales and servicesSub-Major Group632 – Occupations in servicesMinor Group6320 – Cooks, butchers and bakersNOC Code63200

duties: Prepare and cook complete meals or individual dishes and foods.
Prepare and cook special meals for patients as instructed by a dietitian or head chef.
Schedule and supervise kitchen helpers and coordinate activities with wait staff.
Oversee kitchen operations and maintain inventory and records of food and supplies.
May plan menus, determine size of food portions, and estimate food requirements and costs.
Monitor and order supplies and ensure the kitchen and work areas are clean and sanitized.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of a three-year apprenticeship program for cooks OR completion of a college or other program in cooking or food safety is required.
Alternatively, several years of commercial cooking experience may be accepted by employers.
Trade certification is available but voluntary in all provinces and territories.
Red Seal Endorsement is highly recommended for qualified cooks to allow for mobility across Canada.
job_outlook: The outlook for cooks is Favourable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), over 54,000 new job openings are expected over the next decade. This growth is driven by the expansion of the "Quick Service" and "Healthy Casual" dining sectors, as well as a high volume of retirements.
labour_market_demand: The demand for cooks in Canada is currently Very Good, with a critical labor shortage reported in almost every province. The post-pandemic resurgence of tourism and a growing aging population requiring institutional dietary care have made cooks one of the most sought-after trades. Major urban centers like Toronto and Vancouver, as well as rural "work camp" settings, are aggressively recruiting international cooks to fill high-volume vacancies.
High Job Security: The food service industry is one of Canada's largest employers with perpetual demand.
PR Priority: Cooks are frequently included in "Trade" and "Hospitality" specific PNP draws.
Global Exposure: Work with a multicultural workforce and master diverse international cuisines.
Red Seal Advantage: Access to the Red Seal program allows for interprovincial mobility and higher wage potential.
pathways: Federal Skilled Trades (Express Entry): Direct eligibility for cooks with two years of experience.
Canadian Experience Class (CEC): The fastest route for cooks already working in Canada.
Agri-Food Pilot: For cooks working in meat processing or specific food industries.
PNP Hospitality Streams: Provinces like BC and Alberta have dedicated pathways for hospitality workers.
